In Hand #122, I am on the Button (Small Blind) with Queen of Spades and Seven of Hearts. My stack is 15,465 and my opponent, Elon, has 24,385. Q7o is a marginal hand that can sometimes be used as a raise for value or a steal in heads-up play, but given the current game flow where Elon has been active and the stacks are deep, a standard raise to 300 is a reasonable play to try and take the pot early or play with initiative in position.

Preflop in BB facing SB/button raise to 300 (3x). Pot 400, call 200 offers good odds. 8h4h is suited connector with straight/flush potential and postflop playability. Defend wide vs loose SB range. Deep stacks support calling for implied odds. Folding too tight here; raising too strong for this hand.

On the flop of 3h Jh 6c with 8h4h, I have a strong flush draw (9 outs) and decent playability as suited connectors from a wide defending range in BB vs SB/Button open. Deep stacks support semi-bluffing for fold equity and building pot when ahead/draw hits. 2/3 pot bet (400) balances value/thin value and semi-bluff. Checking risks giving free card to overcards/straights that beat me if I hit flush.

On a dry flop of 3h-Jd-6c, Elon has chosen to lead out with a bet of 400 into a pot of 600. My hand, Qs7h, has no direct connection to this board and very little potential to improve. Heads-up preflop on the button/SB vs BB. My hand 8s5h is playable in this spot due to position, deep stacks, and fold equity. Opponent Flash has shown a tendency to fold preflop to raises frequently in the history. Standard GTO play from SB heads-up is to raise a wide range (~70%), including gapped offsuit hands like this for value, position, and steal attempts. Minraise to 2bb (200 total) balances range, denies equity, and pot controls if called. Folding is too tight; limping bad due to OOP disadvantage postflop.

I am in the Big Blind holding King of Diamonds and Queen of Clubs (KQo). Elon, the small blind, has raised to 200. Preflop heads-up on button/SB facing a 3bet to 700 after my minraise to 200. Hand 8s5h is weak offsuit gapped trash with poor playability and low equity vs a 3betting range. Calling commits OOP postflop into deep stacks with a hand that dominates nothing and folds to further pressure. Raising requires strong equity which this lacks. Best to fold and wait for better spots; EV+ long-term discipline over speculative defense.

I am in the Small Blind with 3d 6c, which is a very weak hand. I have a stack of 15,365, while Elon in the Big Blind has 24,485. Given the hand's low potential and being out of position if I call, folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Tc6c is a profitable hand to play heads-up from the SB/Button position. Suited connector with good playability post-flop, potential for straights, flushes, and two-pair combos. Raising steals the dead money in the pot (150) frequently against BB defense ranges, and if called, I have position and can realize equity well. Standard raise size around 3x BB (300 total). Deep stacks allow for post-flop maneuvering. Folding or limping is suboptimal as it misses value and gives opponent initiative.

I am in the Big Blind with Queen-Six offsuit. Elon has raised to 300 from the Small Blind. Given that I will be out of position for the rest of the hand and Q6o is a weak holding that is easily dominated by Elon's opening range, I will fold to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

I am on the button with Ace-Five of diamonds, a premium suited Ace. This is a strong starting hand in heads-up play. I will put in a standard raise to 300 chips to build the pot and exert pressure on Elon in the big blind.

8c4s is a complete trash hand with no high card value, poor connectivity, offsuit, and minimal playability postflop. Heads-up on the button (SB) preflop with Js6d against Flash in the BB. Deep stacks (24k vs 15k), pot 150 after blinds. Limp would be passive and invite multiway pots or raises; folding is too tight late in session with position. Standard raise size 3x BB to 300 total. I am holding 7-5 offsuit in the big blind. Heads-up pre-flop on the button/SB with 5c3s offsuit, a very weak hand with poor playability, low connectivity, and no high card value. I am on the Button (Small Blind) with Queen-Trey offsuit. Heads-up pre-flop from the button/SB with 9h Td offsuit. This is a playable hand with straight potential and decent high-card value in a heads-up spot. I am holding a strong pocket pair (7c, 7d) in the Big Blind. Elon has been opening frequently from the Button/Small Blind. Against a wide opening range in a heads-up situation, pocket sevens are significantly ahead. Facing a 3-bet to 1000 (3.3x) from the BB after my button open to 300. T9o offsuit is a playable hand heads-up in position with straight potential (open-ender frequently), overcard value, and good post-flop playability. BB 3-bet range is polarized: strong value (QQ+, AK) or bluffs/light (suited Ax, suited connectors). T9o has decent equity (~35-40%) against value and crushes bluffs. Position allows us to realize equity well and control pot size. Calling 700 into 2000 pot (~2.85:1 direct odds, better implied) is +EV; we flop well often and can fold to further aggression if missed. Folding too tight given wide button open and BB defense tendencies from history. 4-betting bluff possible but T9o doesn't block top of value range strongly (no A/K/T block really), and pot-committing risky with 14k effective. Shoving absurd. Call to play post-flop optimally.

On a board of Jc Qd 9c, my pocket sevens are in a very vulnerable position. Flopped middle pair (9s) with gutshot straight draw to Broadway (8 outs to straight, plus pair equity) on a coordinated board with two clubs (flush draw possible) and straight draws (e.g., KT, T8s, KTs). ~30-35% equity against villain's 3-bet calling range post-check. Villain checked after 3-bet, which caps his value somewhat (unlikely QQ/JJ/sets always check here) and opens bluff-catching/check-raise range, but we beat Ax high, weaker Qx/Jx, draws, bluffs. Positionally we act first post-flop (SB/Button), so betting takes initiative, protects against turn clubs/straights/Ks, denies equity to pure draws (~12-15% flush draw), extracts value from worse made hands. Pot control via check risks free cards that improve opponent ahead/behind. Standard value/protection bet ~60% pot denies odds and builds when ahead. Size 1200 into 2000 leaves good SPR (~10:1 effective), allows turns/river flexibility. Raising min too small, check too passive given strength.

On this highly coordinated board of Jc-Qd-9c, my pocket sevens have plummeted in value. This texture is extremely favorable for a standard 3-bet calling range, which typically includes many broadway combinations, suited connectors, and potential sets. Given that I am out of position and likely drawing to only two outs, calling here would be a long-term losing play. I will fold to preserve my stack.

I am on the button with Ace-Four offsuit. This is a standard opening hand in heads-up play. I will raise to my standard opening size of 300 to put pressure on the big blind and take control of the hand.

7c3d is an extremely weak hand with no high card value, connectivity, or suitedness. Heads-up pre-flop from the button (SB position), I have position post-flop which is a massive advantage. I am holding Qd 7h in the Big Blind. Elon has raised to 300 from the Button/Small Blind. I am holding Jack of Hearts and Trey of Clubs (J3o), which is a very weak hand with poor post-flop playability. I am holding King-high suited in clubs (Kc 3c) in the Big Blind. Elon raised to 300 from the button, which is a standard opening size. Top pair with Queen kicker (JQ on J73 rainbow board) is very strong heads-up. Dry board with minimal draws (possible gutshots like 45 or 56, but unlikely). I have bottom pair with a King kicker on a very dry board (Jd 7h 3s). Elon's button opening range is wide, and a continuation bet here is standard for him. I have bottom pair (threes) with a King kicker on a relatively dry board of J-7-3-5. On the turn, board is Jd7h3s5d. I have top pair top kicker (Jc pairs Jd, Qd kicker) heads-up against Flash who called preflop from BB and called flop c-bet after checking. I currently hold bottom pair (threes) with a King kicker on a board of J-7-3-5. Elon has fired a second barrel of 1000 into a 1400 pot, which is a significant bet (roughly 71% of the pot). Preflop 3-bet with JJ got called by button raiser, entering flop OOP with top set on K♦ J♦ 5♠ board. I am on the button with a pocket pair of sixes, which is a strong hand in heads-up play. Given my stack size and the current blind levels, I will put in a standard raise to 300 to build the pot and take the initiative against Elon.

AQo is a premium hand from the BB facing a button min-raise from Flash, who raises wide from late position based on hand history. I have pocket sixes, which is a strong hand in heads-up play. Elon has 3-bet my initial raise to 900. Preflop 3-bet with AQo OOP as value hand against button raiser. I have Ace-Seven offsuit on the button in a heads-up situation. This is a strong starting hand for this position and I should raise to build the pot and take control of the hand. My standard open is to 300.

I have 9s 8s, a strong suited connector that plays well post-flop. Elon has been very active, and a 3x raise is a standard opening size. I'll call to see a flop and evaluate, as my hand has good equity against a wide button-opening range.

Heads-up on the button with AdJs after raising preflop and getting called by BB. Flop Ah4hTh is extremely draw-heavy: monotone hearts, potential flush draws, straight draws (e.g., 89, JQ, KQ). On a monotone board of Ace, Ten, and Four of Hearts, I have absolutely no equity with Nine-Eight of Spades. I do not hold a heart, and I have not connected with the board in any way. Jack-Deuce offsuit is a very weak hand, even in a heads-up situation. Given Elon's recent aggression and my position as the shorter stack, there is little reason to commit chips with such a marginal hand. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity in the remaining hands.

Heads-up preflop from SB/button with 64s suited connectors. Excellent postflop playability in position with flush and straight potential. I am in the Big Blind with 9hTh, a strong suited connector that performs well in head-up pots. Elon (the Button/SB) has raised to 300. I am on the button with 9d2c, which is a very weak hand. Heads-up on the button/SB with 7c5c, suited connectors with straight and flush potential. Excellent position and wide raising range preflop in heads-up to steal blinds or play postflop profitably. KJo is a strong starting hand in a heads-up situation, especially when facing a standard button raise. I have 107.65 big blinds, and Elon has a significant lead. On a flop of 4c 6s Tc, I have two overcards (King and Jack). As the Big Blind in a heads-up pot, the standard play is to check to the pre-flop aggressor. Flash checked from BB after calling my button raise preflop, which suggests weakness or trapping, but in position with initiative, this is a clear semi-bluff spot. Betting builds the pot when ahead, applies fold equity to their weak Ax, pair, or draw, and denies them equity on turns that don't help me. Sizing 400 (~2/3 pot) balances value from worse made hands/draws and protection. With position and deep stacks (my 288bb vs their 106bb), continuing aggression maximizes EV over check/call.

On a flop of 4c 6s Tc, I hold two overcards (King and Jack). Elon's continuation bet of 400 into a 600 pot is a standard sizing for him. Given the pot odds (400 to call for a 1400 total pot), I have the required equity to continue, especially since my overcards are likely live against a wide button-opening range. I will call to see a turn and re-evaluate based on the next card and Elon's subsequent action.

The Deuce of Hearts on the turn is essentially a blank card that doesn't change the board texture significantly. I called the flop with two overcards, hoping to improve or reach a cheap showdown. As the out-of-position player, I will continue with the standard play of checking to the aggressor. Leading here doesn't serve much purpose as it's unlikely to fold out better hands or get called by worse, whereas checking allows Elon to continue his aggression with bluffs or check back with marginal hands.

Turn board 2h is a blank for my draws; still have nut flush draw (9 outs) and OESD (8 outs, ~30% equity heads-up). Flash checked flop after calling preflop raise, called my 400 flop bet on coordinated board, and checks turn again, representing possible weakness, medium strength like pair (T,6,4), or weaker draw. With position, massive equity, fold equity against one-pair hands wary of draws, and initiative, semi-bluff betting is +EV: value from worse made hands/draws, protection if ahead, pot building for river shove if improves. Pot 1400, Flash effective ~100BB (10165), bet ~2/3 pot (900) sizes well for value/protection without overbetting, keeps river options open (check/call if checks/brick, bomb if improves). Checking risks giving free card to gutshots/OESD completion or pairing their ace/kq. Late tournament pressure minimal with deep stacks, but aggression exploits history of Flash folding to continued pressure (e.g., hands 20,24,44). High EV play.

On the turn, the board is 4c 6s Tc 2h. I have King-high (Ks Jd), which currently loses to any pair. Elon's turn bet of 900 into a pot of 2300 is roughly 39% of the pot, a smaller sizing than his flop bet. While this sizing could indicate a weak hand or a bluff, calling a second barrel with just two overcards and no significant draw is risky, especially out of position against an aggressive player like Elon. I have about a 13% chance of hitting a King or Jack on the river, which is not enough equity to justify calling the 900 chips (requiring ~28% equity based on pot odds). Preserving my stack for the final few hands is more prudent than making a marginal hero call here.

Holding King-seven offsuit on the button, this is a standard hand to open for a raise in a heads-up or short-handed situation. I want to take the initiative against Elon, who has been playing quite aggressively. Raising to 300 is a standard opening size for this game.

Flash raised from the button/SB, which is a wide range including many suited connectors, broadways, and Ax hands. J9o has decent blockers to top of range (blocks AJ/KQ), straight potential, and solid playability postflop. Facing a 3x BB open effectively, pot odds are excellent (200 to call into ~600 pot post-call). OOP but deep effective stacks (his 9.8k), implied odds good. Calling realizes equity against his wide range while keeping pot small; 3betting risks playing OOP vs calling range or getting stacks in with marginal hand. Folding too tight vs button open. History shows Flash aggressive but folds postflop often. Plan: call and play postflop dynamically.

Flop Ac6d4c is highly coordinated with two clubs for flush draws, possible straight draws (53, 75), and strong Ax value hands. My Js9h has no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw (gutshot or otherwise), just jack-high with minimal backdoor equity. Preflop call was for playability vs wide button range, but flop misses completely. OOP acting first, no reason to donk bet: thin value unlikely, bluffs bad due to poor playability and facing raise/call from Ax/pairs/draws. Flash likely cbets wide (Ax, draws, floats). Plan: check, fold to cbet, preserve stack for better spots. Deep stacks but this is a clear fold equity realization spot post-check.

I opened King-Seven offsuit from the button and Elon called. The flop comes Ace-high with two clubs. As the pre-flop aggressor, an Ace-high board is very favorable for my perceived range. I have the King of clubs, which provides a small amount of backdoor equity and serves as a blocker to some of Elon's flush draws. A continuation bet here is standard to put pressure on his marginal hands and to take down the pot immediately. I'll bet half the pot.

Js9h has zero showdown value or draws on Ac6d4c flop (flush-heavy, straight possible). Preflop call was speculative for playability vs wide button range, but flop miss warrants check-fold OOP vs cbet. Pot odds ~3:1 but no equity realization; villain's wide cbet range crushes jack-high. Raising lacks fold equity OOP with air and poor playability. Deep stacks but folding preserves BB for final hands to maximize stack. History confirms Flash cbets wide but value hands continue.

5h4d is a very weak offsuit hand with poor equity against a big blind defending range and limited postflop playability, especially out of position if called or 3-bet. Deep stacks (my 29k vs opponent's 10k) and pot odds (50 to call into 150) are tempting but not enough given the hand's trash nature. In heads-up from SB/Button, opening range is wide, but 54o is bottom of range. Prefer to fold and wait for a better spot with only 4 hands left to maximize stack by avoiding marginal situations.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Five offsuit. This is a very weak hand, and given Elon's recent aggression in the Big Blind, it is not worth a raise to steal. Folding is the most prudent play to conserve my stack as we approach the end of the match.

Heads-up preflop on the button/SB vs BB. My stack is deep at ~295 BB effective (opponent has 103 BB). T6o is within a standard heads-up opening range (~40-60% of hands), with straight potential and decent playability postflop from position. Raising steals the dead money in the pot (150 already) frequently, as BB defends wide based on history. Standard size 3x BB.

We are at hand 149 of 150, and I am significantly behind in total chips. Elon has been very aggressive from the button throughout the match. Jack-Seven offsuit is a weak hand to defend against a 3x raise out of position, especially when I need to preserve my stack for a potentially better spot in the final hand to minimize the overall deficit. Pot odds are 3:1, but the playability of J7o post-flop is poor.

On the final hand of the match, I find myself on the button with an Ace. In a heads-up scenario, any Ace is a strong starting hand and should be played aggressively. I will raise to 300 to take the initiative and build a pot, looking to finish the session on a positive note.

Heads-up preflop in BB vs SB button raise to 300 (standard steal attempt given position). Qc8d is a decent speculative offsuit hand with queen blocker, good playability, and multiway it would be a fold but heads-up vs wide range it's profitable to defend wide OOP due to excellent pot odds (200 to call into 400 pot, ~3:1). Equity realization postflop ~40%+ vs typical SB range (30-50% hands). Position is lost but stacks deep (295BB effective vs villain's 100BB), last hand so no ICM/future play concerns. Folding too tight here denies equity; raising thin OOP risks domination; call to realize equity and control pot OOP with check-calls.

Flop completely misses Qc8d: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot to 9T but unlikely), no flush draw. Queen-high air OOP vs button wide raising range (30-50% hands). Board connected with heart flush draw, straight draws (45s,58s,89s, A5 flush draw possible). Villain cbets wide ~60-70% polarized (value: sets, two-pair 76/65, trips 2/6/7; bluffs: Ahxh, overcards, missed Ax). Check-fold optimal: folding to cbet denies equity realization OOP where check-calling air loses to value/overbets, check-raising too thin without fold equity blockers (no A,K). If villain checks back (10-20%), free river card to improve or showdown. Last hand no future play/ICM pressure. Deep effective (100BB villain) favors pot control/check.

On a flop of 2h-7s-6h, I have bottom pair (Deuces) with an Ace kicker and the Ace of hearts for a backdoor nut flush draw. In heads-up play, a pair is a relatively strong holding. Since Elon checked, I will place a continuation bet of 300 (half the pot) to extract value from draws (like 89, 54, or hearts) and overcards, while also protecting my hand from giving a free card.

Qc8d is complete air on 276 rainbow-ish board (two hearts) with straight draws and flush draws possible. OOP vs button continuation bet into decent sized pot (300 into 600, now 300 into 900). Villain's range wide preflop (~40% button opens), cbets polarized: value (sets 22/66/77, two-pair 76/65/26s, overpairs) and bluffs (Ahxh flush draws, missed overcards, straight draws like 45/58/89). Queen-high has minimal showdown value and poor playability OOP multi-street; check-call risks bloating pot with worse, check-raise too thin without blockers or fold equity. Pot odds ~4:1 superficially good but unrealized equity low (~15-20% vs range) and tough spots ahead. Last hand, no future play concerns, stacks effective 97BB villain deeper irrelevant for air. Standard check-fold preserves stack maximization.
